key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Responsible Business Conduct (Amendment) Directions, 2026
*(RBI/2026-27/74, dated 2026-04-29; effective 2026-07-01)*

- **What it does:** Inserts **Part B – "Measures in case of declaration of calamity"** into **Chapter IV (Miscellaneous Instructions)** of the Responsible Business Conduct Directions, 2025 (RBI/2026-27/74, 2026-04-29).
- **New provision (para 33A):** An AIFI **may, at its discretion**, provide relief measures such as **waiver / reduction of various fees and charges** for customers in areas where a **calamity has been declared**, for a period **not exceeding one year**.
- **Nature:** Enabling/discretionary — it permits relief, it does not mandate it.
- **Commencement:** **July 1, 2026.**

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/2026-27/74, dated 2026-04-29 · effective 2026-07-01)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Chapter IV – Part B "Measures in case of declaration of calamity" (para 33A)** | **Inserted** | AIFIs may, at discretion, waive/reduce fees and charges for customers in calamity-declared areas, for up to one year. |

- **Effective date:** July 1, 2026.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** This gives NaBFID an explicit, RBI-sanctioned basis to ease the cost burden on borrowers hit by a declared calamity — a customer-protection lever consistent with responsible-conduct expectations.

*Illustrative example:* If a district hosting a NaBFID-financed project is officially declared calamity-affected, NaBFID may waive or reduce processing/penal charges for the affected borrower for up to one year — but should document the board-approved policy basis, since the relief is discretionary.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
